author: Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani
description: The most authoritative and comprehensive commentary on Sahih al-Bukhari, written by the master hadith scholar Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani. Considered the definitive reference for understanding Bukhari's hadith.

author: Imam al-Nawawi
description: The principal commentary on Sahih Muslim by Imam Yahya ibn Sharaf al-Nawawi, recognized for clarity, depth, and linguistic precision. Standard reference in Islamic scholarship.

author: al-Mubarakpuri
description: The standard commentary on Jami al-Tirmidhi, known for its precise analysis of hadith grades and scholarly opinions on fiqh derived from each hadith.

author: al-Azimabadi
description: Comprehensive commentary on Sunan Abu Dawud, covering fiqh implications, linguistic analysis, and scholarly positions on each hadith.

author: Ibn Allan al-Siddiqi
description: The most widely used classical commentary on Riyadh us-Salihin, covering linguistic analysis, fiqh rulings, and spiritual reflections on each hadith.

author: Ibn Uthaymin
description: Modern scholarly commentary by Sheikh Muhammad ibn Salih al-Uthaymin, noted for its accessibility, contemporary relevance, and Athari methodology. Widely used in Islamic study circles worldwide.

author: al-Sanani
description: The principal commentary on Bulugh al-Maram, explaining fiqh derivations from each hadith with reference to the four major schools.

author: IslamWiki Scholarly Team
description: Concise educational explanations synthesized from classical commentary works, designed to make hadith meanings accessible while maintaining scholarly accuracy. Draws primarily from Fath al-Bari, Sharh al-Nawawi, and Ibn Uthaymin's commentaries.
